title: Comic Panel Breakdown and Beat Adaptation
summary: Rules for translating a story into comic pages by adapting beats, assigning one dominant change per panel, and preserving continuity and inference across gutters.

rules.title: Adapt beats, not sentences
rules.instruction: Scenes and panels should adapt story beats and functions, not split prose mechanically sentence by sentence.
rules.failureMessage: Panel planning appears to split text instead of adapting story beats.

rules.title: Each panel has one dominant change
rules.instruction: Every panel must create a visible story, emotional, spatial, or medical change from its start state to end state.
rules.failureMessage: A panel does not create a readable state change.

rules.title: Pages carry rhythm and page-ending effects
rules.instruction: Page purpose and page-ending effect should shape panel order, pacing, reveals, and cliffhangers.
rules.failureMessage: Panel planning lacks page-level rhythm or page-ending intent.

rules.title: Gutters imply intentional inference
rules.instruction: Panel transitions must make clear what the reader infers between moments instead of hiding necessary action or medical causality.
rules.failureMessage: A gutter transition leaves required causality or action unreadable.

rules.title: Track continuity explicitly
rules.instruction: Panel planning must track continuity for characters, props, locations, time, costumes, injuries, and medical devices.
rules.failureMessage: Continuity anchors or ledger entries are missing for repeated story elements.

rules.title: Avoid text-image redundancy
rules.instruction: Do not use lettering to restate exactly what the image already shows unless the repetition has a specific instructional purpose.
rules.failureMessage: A panel uses text and image redundantly without a stated purpose.

items.label: Story spine and scene function are present.
Every panel has start state, end state, and change created.
Panel transitions preserve causality and reader inference.
Continuity ledger covers characters, props, locations, time, costumes, injuries, and medical devices.
Page purpose and page-ending effects are explicit.

gateCriteria.blockingWhen: Any panel has no dominant change, missing continuity anchors, or unclear causality.
gateCriteria.failureMessage: Panel planning cannot proceed to render prep until blocking adaptation issues are resolved.
